title: 𝑨 𝒇𝒖𝒍𝒍𝒚 𝒇𝒖𝒏𝒅𝒆𝒅 𝑷𝒉𝑫 𝒑𝒐𝒔𝒊𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏 𝒊𝒔 𝒂𝒗𝒂𝒊𝒍𝒂𝒃𝒍𝒆 𝒂𝒕 𝒕𝒉𝒆 University of North Carolina at Charlotte 𝒊𝒏 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝑪𝒐𝒍𝒍𝒆𝒈𝒆 𝒐𝒇 𝑪𝒐𝒎𝒑𝒖𝒕𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝑰𝒏𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒎𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒄𝒔.
institution: 𝑨 𝒇𝒖𝒍𝒍𝒚 𝒇𝒖𝒏𝒅𝒆𝒅 𝑷𝒉𝑫 𝒑𝒐𝒔𝒊𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏 𝒊𝒔 𝒂𝒗𝒂𝒊𝒍𝒂𝒃𝒍𝒆 𝒂𝒕 𝒕𝒉𝒆 University of North Carolina at Charlotte 𝒊𝒏 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝑪𝒐𝒍𝒍𝒆𝒈𝒆 𝒐𝒇 𝑪𝒐𝒎𝒑𝒖𝒕𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝑰𝒏𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒎𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒄𝒔.
content:
𝑨 𝒇𝒖𝒍𝒍𝒚 𝒇𝒖𝒏𝒅𝒆𝒅 𝑷𝒉𝑫 𝒑𝒐𝒔𝒊𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏 𝒊𝒔 𝒂𝒗𝒂𝒊𝒍𝒂𝒃𝒍𝒆 𝒂𝒕 𝒕𝒉𝒆 University of North Carolina at Charlotte 𝒊𝒏 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝑪𝒐𝒍𝒍𝒆𝒈𝒆 𝒐𝒇 𝑪𝒐𝒎𝒑𝒖𝒕𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝑰𝒏𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒎𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒄𝒔.

The position is available for Spring 2027 or Fall 2027. Applicants looking for a Fall 2026 start may also mention this in their email.

The position focuses on research in online learning and decision-making, especially in the areas of Generative AI, reliable AI evaluation, calibration, real-world decision systems, and machine learning for business.

The research will involve developing methods for sequential decision-making in GenAI, evaluating and calibrating reliable AI systems, and applying machine learning to real-world business problems such as dynamic pricing.

Possible research topics include sequential decision-making in GenAI, reliable AI evaluation and calibration, online learning, decision-making systems, machine learning for business, dynamic pricing, and real-world AI systems.

Applicants with strong foundations in mathematics, coding, machine learning, artificial intelligence, data science, or related areas are encouraged to apply. Strong programming, research, and problem-solving skills are highly valued.

Prior publications are welcome but not required. Preference will be given to applicants who have seriously engaged with a research problem or substantial project and can clearly explain the motivation, methods, challenges, and their own contributions.

Admitted PhD students will receive financial support, including stipend, tuition, and insurance, subject to university and program policies.

Interested applicants should send the following materials:

CV
Transcript
A short note of one or two paragraphs on research interests

Application materials should be sent to:

jxu22@charlotte.edu

Email subject:

[PhD Application] – Your Name

Please share with anyone who may be a good fit.
